summary: Batch Upload Feature Testing Complete. All 8 backend API tests passed. All 12 frontend UI elements verified. The batch upload feature is fully functional with proper tab navigation, category selection, pairing modes, and batch-save endpoint working correctly.
passed_tests: BACKEND - POST /api/inventory/batch-save with empty cards array returns {saved: 0, total: 0, errors: []}
BACKEND - POST /api/inventory/batch-save creates single card in collection category
BACKEND - POST /api/inventory/batch-save creates multiple cards in for_sale category
BACKEND - POST /api/inventory/batch-save accepts all optional fields (variation, grading_company, grade, purchase_price, notes, sport)
BACKEND - POST /api/cards/identify endpoint exists and requires image_base64
BACKEND - POST /api/cards/identify accepts and processes image_base64 parameter
BACKEND - Batch saved cards appear in inventory via GET /api/inventory
FRONTEND - Batch Upload tab visible alongside My Cards and Scan Card tabs
FRONTEND - Batch Upload tab has data-testid='inv-tab-batch'
FRONTEND - Collection category button with data-testid='batch-category-collection'
FRONTEND - For Sale category button with data-testid='batch-category-for_sale'
FRONTEND - Front+Back (Alternating) pairing mode with data-testid='pairing-alternating'
FRONTEND - Front Only pairing mode with data-testid='pairing-front-only'
FRONTEND - Drag & drop zone with data-testid='batch-drop-zone'
FRONTEND - Back button returns to My Cards view correctly
critical_code_review_comments: BatchSaveRequest model (line 3854) correctly accepts cards array and category

BatchCardItem model (line 3837) has all required fields including card_name, player, year, set_name, card_number, variation, condition, grading_company, grade, purchase_price, quantity, notes, image_base64, back_image_base64, sport
batch_save_inventory endpoint (line 3858) properly iterates through cards, processes images with process_card_image(), and returns saved/total/errors
BatchUploadView.jsx properly implements step indicator, category selector, pairing modes, and drag-drop zone
InventoryModule.jsx line 541 correctly includes 'batch' tab with Batch Upload label
seed_data_creation: Test data created and cleaned up during testing. Current inventory has 4 cards.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Batch Upload feature fully tested and working. Key data-testid values: inv-tab-batch (tab), batch-category-collection, batch-category-for_sale, pairing-alternating, pairing-front-only, batch-drop-zone, batch-view-back-btn, batch-identify-btn. The /api/inventory/batch-save endpoint accepts BatchSaveRequest with cards array and category field. Each card in the array uses BatchCardItem model. The /api/cards/identify endpoint works for AI card identification.

features_verified.batch_upload_tab_visible: PASS - Tab appears alongside My Cards and Scan Card
features_verified.step_indicators: PASS - Upload and Review & Save steps visible
features_verified.category_selector: PASS - Collection and For Sale buttons with data-testid attributes
features_verified.pairing_mode_selector: PASS - Front+Back Alternating and Front Only modes with data-testid attributes
features_verified.back_button: PASS - Returns to My Cards view
features_verified.batch_save_single: PASS - Creates single card in collection
features_verified.batch_save_multiple: PASS - Creates multiple cards in for_sale
